skill.name: Database Admin
skill.description: Expert database administrator specializing in modern cloud databases, automation, and reliability engineering. Masters AWS/Azure/GCP database services, Infrastructure as Code, high availability, disaster recovery, performance optimization, and compliance.
skill.summary: Expert database administrator for cloud-native databases, automation, and reliability engineering across multi-cloud environments.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only skill with no executable code. Static analysis scanned 1 file (SKILL.md) containing only markdown documentation and instructions. No security risks detected. The skill provides database administration expertise through conversational guidance without any code execution, network access, or file system operations.

content.user_title: Master database operations and cloud infrastructure
content.value_statement: Managing modern cloud databases across multiple platforms requires deep expertise and constant vigilance. This skill provides expert guidance for database architecture, automation, high availability, disaster recovery, and compliance across AWS, Azure, and GCP.
actual_capabilities: Design multi-cloud database architectures with high availability and disaster recovery
Implement Infrastructure as Code for database provisioning using Terraform and CloudFormation
Configure comprehensive monitoring, alerting, and performance optimization
Create automated backup and recovery procedures with point-in-time recovery
Ensure database security compliance for HIPAA, PCI-DSS, SOX, and GDPR
Optimize database costs while maintaining performance SLAs
limitations: Cannot directly execute database commands or connect to live database instances
Cannot perform actual database migrations or schema changes automatically
Cannot access external monitoring systems or cloud provider consoles directly
Recommendations require manual implementation by users with appropriate access credentials

use_cases.title: Multi-Region Database Architecture
use_cases.description: Design a PostgreSQL setup with automated failover across AWS, Azure, and GCP regions with defined RPO and RTO objectives
use_cases.target_user: DevOps engineers building resilient database infrastructure

use_cases.title: Database Security Compliance
use_cases.description: Implement database security controls and audit procedures meeting HIPAA compliance requirements with encryption and access management

use_cases.title: Cost Optimization Review
use_cases.description: Analyze database spending across cloud providers and recommend right-sizing, reserved capacity, and storage tiering strategies
use_cases.target_user: FinOps analysts and engineering managers controlling cloud costs

prompt_templates.title: Database Health Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Review our PostgreSQL database configuration and identify performance bottlenecks. Current setup: 8GB RAM, 4 vCPU, 500GB storage, 200 concurrent connections. Peak CPU usage reaches 85% daily. What optimizations do you recommend?

prompt_templates.title: Backup Strategy Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a comprehensive backup and disaster recovery strategy for our MySQL database running on AWS RDS. Requirements: RPO of 1 hour, RTO of 4 hours, cross-region failover capability, compliance with SOX audit requirements. Include automation approach using Terraform.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Cloud Database Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a zero-downtime migration from Azure SQL Database to Google Cloud Spanner for a global application. Current database: 2TB, 10,000 transactions per second, 99.99% SLA requirement. Include data synchronization strategy, cutover approach, rollback procedures, and monitoring during migration.

prompt_templates.title: Database Security Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a database security architecture for a healthcare application requiring HIPAA compliance. Include encryption at rest and in transit, RBAC implementation, audit logging, secret rotation, vulnerability scanning, and network isolation. Provide Terraform code structure for AWS RDS with PostgreSQL.

output_examples.input: Design a high availability setup for PostgreSQL on AWS with automatic failover
output: Architecture Overview: Multi-AZ PostgreSQL deployment using Amazon RDS with read replicas in each availability zone
Failover Configuration: Automated failover enabled with 30-second detection window and DNS-based traffic routing
Monitoring Setup: CloudWatch alarms for CPU > 80%, connections > 80%, replication lag > 60 seconds
Backup Strategy: Automated daily snapshots with 30-day retention, point-in-time recovery enabled
Implementation Steps: Create RDS subnet group, enable multi-AZ, configure read replicas, set up CloudWatch alarms, test failover procedure

output_examples.input: How do I optimize query performance for a slow-running dashboard?
output: Analysis Approach: Enable slow query log, identify queries exceeding 1 second execution time
Index Recommendations: Create composite indexes on frequently filtered columns, cover index for dashboard queries
Query Optimization: Add missing WHERE clause filters, eliminate SELECT *, use EXPLAIN ANALYZE for query plans
Connection Pooling: Implement PgBouncer with transaction pooling mode, set pool size to 2x CPU cores
Caching Layer: Add Redis cache for dashboard aggregations with 5-minute TTL to reduce database load

best_practices: Always test backup restoration procedures monthly to verify recovery capability and document any issues
Implement Infrastructure as Code for all database changes to ensure consistency and enable version control
Monitor replication lag, connection pool utilization, and query performance metrics proactively before incidents occur
anti_patterns: Running production databases on single instances without multi-AZ or replication for high availability
Storing database credentials in application code or configuration files instead of using secret management services
Skipping backup verification and assuming backups work without regularly testing restoration procedures

faq.question: What cloud database platforms does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill covers AWS (RDS, Aurora, DynamoDB, DocumentDB), Azure (SQL Database, PostgreSQL, MySQL, Cosmos DB), and Google Cloud (Cloud SQL, Spanner, Firestore, BigQuery). It also addresses multi-cloud strategies and cross-platform database operations.

faq.question: Can this skill directly connect to my database and run queries?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides expert guidance, architecture design, and implementation instructions but cannot directly connect to or execute commands on your databases. All recommendations must be implemented manually by users with appropriate access.

faq.question: How does this skill help with database compliance requirements?
faq.answer: The skill provides guidance on implementing security controls, audit logging, encryption, access management, and documentation required for HIPAA, PCI-DSS, SOX, and GDPR compliance. It outlines architectural patterns and configuration recommendations that meet regulatory requirements.

faq.question: What Infrastructure as Code tools are supported for database automation?
faq.answer: The skill covers Terraform, CloudFormation, and ARM templates for database provisioning. It also addresses schema management tools like Flyway and Liquibase, configuration management with Ansible and Chef, and GitOps workflows for database changes.

faq.question: Can this skill help with database cost optimization?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill provides strategies for right-sizing instances, implementing reserved capacity, storage tiering, query optimization to reduce resource consumption, and cross-cloud cost comparison for workload placement decisions.

faq.question: What disaster recovery options does this skill cover?
faq.answer: The skill addresses multi-region replication, automated and manual failover procedures, backup strategies including full and incremental backups, point-in-time recovery, cross-region disaster recovery, and chaos engineering for resilience testing.
